subject: english
title: Tiếng Anh Marie Curie 2024 (đề chính thức)
intro: Đề thi chính thức — Đánh giá năng lực vào lớp 6 Trường Marie Curie, môn Tiếng Anh. 60 câu trắc nghiệm.
source: Đề thi chính thức Marie Curie 2024 (MS MY English Center / Cô My Tiếng Anh)
_answerNote: Ảnh đề KHÔNG kèm bảng đáp án. Toàn bộ đáp án do Claude tự giải (câu khách quan: ngữ âm/trọng âm/ngữ pháp/từ vựng/đọc hiểu — độ tin cậy cao). CẦN đối chiếu key chính thức. Hai câu sắp xếp có nhiều phương án đúng ngữ pháp: câu 20 (C 'My father is my biggest inspiration' và D 'My biggest inspiration is my father' đều đúng — chọn C). Còn lại độ tin cậy cao.

passages.title: A very clever family
passages.body:
The Smiths are possibly the cleverest family in the country, and now the youngest member, ten-year-old Charlotte, has won a national spelling competition to add to the family's successes. Charlotte is the youngest ever winner of the National Young Spelling Bee Competition – the youngest winner before her was her older sister, Helen, who won it when she was eleven.

Charlotte and Helen's older brother, Mark, is also very clever. He finished school early and went to university at the age of fifteen, three years before most young people start their university studies. The children's parents, Charles and Vivien, are both teachers and say their children's success comes from working hard, playing hard and following strict rules about homework and bedtimes.

'Other people think that Charles and I don't let the children have any time to relax and play, and that we're always making them do their homework. But it's not true!' says Vivien. 'We have lots of fun in the family. But there's a time for fun and there's a time for work, and we make sure the children understand that work comes before play.'

When Charlotte took part in the spelling competition, the whole family, including her grandparents, went to watch. Helen took off a day from school – the first day in her life that she missed going to school. 'I felt bad about missing school,' Helen said, 'but I think it was important to Charlotte that I was there.'

Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: cousin / My / caves / likes / exploring /.
Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: a / toys / She / to / second-hand / old / took / all /.

options.text: She took all old toys to her second-hand shop.
She took all her second-hand toys to a old shop.
She took all her old toys to a second-hand shop.
She all took her old toys to a second-hand shop.

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: army / grandfather / ever / your / Was / in / the /?

options.text: Was the grandfather ever in your army?
Was your grandfather ever in the army?
Was your grandfather in the ever army?
Was the army your grandfather ever in?

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: vacation / How / on / family / often / go / your / does /?

options.text: How often does your family go on vacation?
How your family does often go on vacation?
How does your family go on vacation often?
How often your family does go on vacation?

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: move / the / I / countryside / in / want / of / city / to / the / out / and / live /.

options.text: I want to move the city and out of live in the countryside.
I want to move in the city and live the countryside out of.
I want to live out of the city and move in the countryside.
I want to move out of the city and live in the countryside.

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: species / animal / today / endangered / than / ten / ago / years / More / are /.

options.text: More endangered animal species are today than ten years ago.
More animal species are endangered today than ten years ago.
More species are endangered today than animal ten years ago.
More animal species are endangered ten years ago than today.

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: here / near / any / good / eat / to / places / Are / there /?

options.text: Are here any good places to eat near there?
Are there good places to eat any near here?
Are any good places to eat there near there?
Are there any good places to eat near here?

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: singing / I'm / brother / not / good / as / my / at / as /.

options.text: I'm not as good at singing as my brother.
I'm not as good as singing at my brother.
I'm as good at singing as not my brother.
I'm not as good as at singing my brother.

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: some / like / Would / dessert / you / more /?

options.text: Would you some dessert like more?
Would you like dessert some more?
Would you like some more dessert?
Would you like more dessert some?

questions.stem: Reorder the words to make a correct sentence: inspiration / is / My / father / biggest / my /.

options.text: My inspiration is my biggest father.
My father is my inspiration biggest.
My father is my biggest inspiration.
My biggest inspiration is my father.

questions.stem: The ice cream man parked the van and waited for ______ customers.

- What did Emma do last night?

______ to Singapore?

I ______ your book all day. It's very interesting, but I'm only on chapter 2.

What word can be removed from this sentence without changing its meaning?
"The Wimbledon men's final was the best game of tennis that I've ever seen."
Has Susan ever ______ a cowboy hat?
______ I work, ______ time I spend with my family.

"Are you coming to the party?", Maria asked.
Maria asked Izzie if ______.

options.text: she will come to the party
she is coming to the party
she was coming to the party

questions.stem: I don't spend a lot of time ______ computer games.
They took her to the hospital immediately. That spider ______ poisonous.
We ______ on our project this time tomorrow.

options.text: when she was older than Helen

questions.stem: Most people ______.

options.text: are like Mark when they go to university
start university at the age of eighteen

questions.stem: Charles and Vivien help their children by ______.

options.text: letting them relax and play any time they want
making them do their homework before they relax
not letting them have any time to relax and play
making them relax before they do their homework

questions.stem: When Charlotte won the competition, ______.

options.text: Helen missed it because she was at school
all the family was there, but not her grandparents
her brother, sister, parents, and grandparents were watching
the rest of her family were working

questions.stem: Helen said that ______.

options.text: Charlotte wanted her to be at the competition
Charlotte missed school for the competition
Charlotte thought competitions were more important than school
